meaning: The way in which the parts of something are arranged or laid out.
example: The magazine has a clean and modern layout.
example2: He changed the layout of the furniture in his room.
example3: The factory's layout is designed for efficiency.
Explanation: A 'layout' is a noun referring to the specific arrangement or plan of elements within a defined space. It is crucial in design (page layout), architecture (building layout), urban planning (city layout), and manufacturing (plant layout). A good layout is functional, aesthetically pleasing, and serves its purpose efficiently. The word often implies a deliberate, planned configuration rather than a random one. The verb phrase is 'lay out'.
usageTip: Use 'layout' to talk about the organized plan or arrangement of physical or visual elements.
commonMistake: Spelling it as 'lay out' when used as a noun. The noun is one word: 'layout'.

meaning: The way in which text, images, or components are arranged on a page or screen
example: The website layout looks great on mobile phones.
example2: We need to change the layout of the dashboard to show more data.
example3: The keyboard layout is set to 'US English' by default.
Explanation: Layout is the noun that describes the final arrangement of things. In web development, it involves using HTML and CSS to create a structure. A good layout makes a website easy to use and navigate. It is the 'map' of where everything goes. Designers spend a lot of time testing different layouts to see which one users like best. It is one word when used as a noun.
usageTip: Use 'layout' to refer to the structure of a page or a keyboard.
commonMistake: Using 'lay out' (two words) when you are referring to the design itself.
